intent.oneSentenceIntent: A simple, fast batch file renamer that lets you preview and undo before touching any file.
targetUsers: a Mac user who needs to rename many files at once without fear
intent.problem: renaming files in bulk is risky and Finder's tools are limited
intent.desiredOutcome: rename a batch of files safely, seeing every change before it happens and able to undo it
explicitConstraints: keep it simple and fast, not a power-user sprawl

coreJourneys.trigger: user drops a folder of files onto the app
preconditions: the user picked the folder via the open panel
steps: load the files into a table
type a find/replace or pick a rule
review the live preview with changed parts highlighted
coreJourneys.expectedOutcome: the files are renamed exactly as previewed
coreJourneys.failureRecovery: if two files would collide, Apply is blocked before any change; after Apply, Undo restores every original name

sources.title: user asked for a simple, fast batch file renamer

analogClaims.claim: well-regarded renamers compute a live preview as a pure function of the rules, detect conflicts before apply, and keep a full undo stack

baseline.statement: show a live preview of new names, with changed parts highlighted, before anything is applied
provide a full undo that restores every original name after an applied batch
detect name conflicts and block Apply before any file is changed
support regex find & replace plus common rules (numbering, case, replace)

customDelta.statement: stay simple and fast — a focused rule set, not a power-user sprawl

dataFlows.dataCategory: file names and metadata in user-selected folders
dataFlows.retention: not retained — read into memory, written back on Apply
dataFlows.deletion: n/a (no separate store of file data)

capabilityNeeds.capability: read-write file access to user-selected folders
capabilityNeeds.minimalScope: only folders the user picks, via the open panel + security-scoped bookmarks
capabilityNeeds.purpose: read names and rename files in place

successCriteria: user previews every new name before any file is touched
user can fully undo an applied batch
no file is overwritten because of a name conflict

then: new names update live with changes highlighted

acceptanceTests.given: a rule that maps two files to the same name

unknowns.description: should it support metadata (EXIF/audio) renaming, or stay simple?

unresolvedRisks: renaming is destructive — undo and conflict detection are mandatory, not optional

decisionLog.decision: explicit Apply gate; never rename on keystroke
decisionLog.rationale: a destructive op must be gated behind preview; UI/UX decided for the user

decisionLog.decision: file access scoped to user-selected folders
decisionLog.rationale: minimal capability logically required by the request; confirmed by request

stopConditions: any requirement implies a network call
verificationEvidenceRequired: automated assertion for undo and conflict blocking
